sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current ring: g, S, b, p, Y, g, p, b, S, g, p, S, b, b, w, w, g, p, S, w
# Yellow is at slot 4
# Let me try rotating CCW 4 more times to get yellow to slot 0
# Then check if the level completes

# Batch 4 CCW rotations (click RED 4 times)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 5}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 5}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 5}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 5}])

# Check result
ring_slots = [
    (20, 14), (20, 20), (20, 26), (20, 32), (20, 38), (20, 44), (20, 50),
    (26, 50), (32, 50), (38, 50), (44, 50),
    (44, 44), (44, 38), (44, 32), (44, 26), (44, 20), (44, 14),
    (38, 14), (32, 14), (26, 14),
]

def get_ring_colors(seg):
    colors = []
    for r, c in ring_slots:
        for n in seg['nodes']:
            if n['pixels'] == 16:
                bd = n['boundary']
                cr = sum(p[0] for p in bd) / len(bd)
                cc = sum(p[1] for p in bd) / len(bd)
                if abs(cr - r) < 2 and abs(cc - c) < 2:
                    colors.append(n['color'])
                    break
        else:
            colors.append('?')
    return colors

current_colors = get_ring_colors(current_frame.segmentation)
print("After 4 more CCW rotations:", ' -> '.join(current_colors))
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Score: {last_action_result.get('score', 'unknown')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
